_comment: Curated handles whose posts can be auto-amplified (reposted) by Parliament Audit without per-post editorial review. Add to this list to expand the trust boundary; remove to tighten it. Daily-ops picks up to 2 candidates per day from these accounts whose posts (a) are < 24 hours old, (b) match at least one keywords_positive term from social-brief/watchlist.json, and (c) don't trigger any keywords_skip. Reposts attach the original poster's text; we add no commentary.

trusted.name: Philippe J. Fournier
trusted.rationale: Non-partisan electoral-projection model; evidence-first content; no opinion grandstanding.

trusted.name: House of Commons
trusted.rationale: Primary source. Procedural updates, Hansard pointers.

trusted.name: Aaron Wherry
trusted.rationale: Veteran press-gallery wire reporter; factual feed.

trusted.name: Althia Raj
trusted.rationale: Press-gallery columnist; mostly factual on Bluesky.

trusted.name: Rosemary Barton
trusted.rationale: CBC chief political correspondent; on-record reporting.

trusted.name: Democracy Watch
trusted.rationale: Non-partisan accountability watchdog; cited in our own d'Entremont piece.

_excluded_intentionally: @taxpayerDOTcom — high engagement but advocacy framing, not non-partisan

x._note: X-side amplification is currently disabled because (a) the Pay Per Use API tier doesn't include the read endpoints needed to find candidate posts safely, and (b) browser-scraping X for amplification candidates introduces brittleness. When ready, mirror the Bluesky list here.
